description: Someone who answers 'towe' reports an overall good state in the frame that matters. The same adjective can describe a community's welfare or compare how people and groups fare without reducing the judgement to one symptom. Underlying health is 'sunai', and 'wolu' (healthy) describes a person or system in that condition. A person may have an ongoing condition and still answer 'towe' when they are well today.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into stressed true-mid back rounded 'o'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into true-mid front 'e'.
sound_symbolism: The dental stop gives 'towe' a firm beginning, then rounded 'o' opens forward through 'we'. The compact move from contact into open voice feels settled without going flat.
usage_notes: 'towe' usually predicates with 'nai'. The quality-noun rule also permits 'towe' with 'phaelo' (feel), as in 'mia towe phaelo' (I feel well).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: 'su theula towe nai' wishes that all be well. 'towe' states the condition being wished for without claiming to know one form of wellness for every person.

semantic_domains.community: the overall welfare of a person or group
semantic_domains.physical: a present report that one's bodily condition is good
